Title: Cell-type-specific control of secondary cell wall formation by Musashi-type translational regulators in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Musashi-like RNA-binding protein; two N-terminal RNA recognition motifs (RRM1 and RRM2) and intrinsically disordered C-terminus
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm; recruited to stress granules under heat stress
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MSIL4; PAB2; PAB4; PAB8; RBP45/47; EIF4G; RBGD2; RBGD4
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MSIL2 functions redundantly with MSIL4 to control secondary cell wall formation in Arabidopsis interfascicular fibers. 2. Loss of MSIL2 together with MSIL4 reduces lignin deposition, alters cellulose-related staining, increases biomass saccharification, and increases glucuronoxylan 4-O-methylation. 3. MSIL2/4 regulate SCW biosynthesis primarily post-transcriptionally/translationally rather than through major transcript-level changes in SCW genes.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Lignin; glucuronoxylan with 4-O-methylated glucuronic acid decorations
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal stem development; heat stress used for stress-granule localization assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pendant inflorescence stem; thinner interfascicular fiber secondary walls; altered wall architecture and reduced biomass recalcitrance
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Secondary cell wall mechanical strength, stem rigidity, water-conducting tissue support, and biomass recalcitrance
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MSIL2 promoter for genomic tagged complementation/localization constructs
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant analysis; complementation with tagged genomic constructs; fluorescence localization; AP-MS interactome; stem histology; lignin quantification; FTIR; saccharification; RNA-seq; quantitative proteomics
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Widely expressed; enriched in secondary-cell-wall-forming interfascicular fiber and xylem vessel cells in inflorescence stem
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MSIL2 alone shows no obvious phenotype; functional effect is redundant with MSIL4. The study supports translational repression of SCW-related mRNAs by MSIL2/4 but direct RNA-IP evidence was shown specifically for GXM1/3 with MSIL4.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Interfascicular fiber secondary cell walls of inflorescence stem
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes proper secondary cell wall formation and lignin deposition while restraining excess glucuronoxylan methylation
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GEO GSE223732; quantitative proteomics: ProteomeXchange PXD040207; PXD040020
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed; high in interfascicular fiber and xylem vessel cells

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Musashi-like RNA-binding protein; two N-terminal RNA recognition motifs (RRM1 and RRM2) and intrinsically disordered C-terminus
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Cytoplasm; recruited to stress granules under heat stress
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MSIL2; PAB2; PAB4; PAB8; RBP45/47; EIF4G; RBGD2; RBGD4; GXM1 mRNA; GXM3 mRNA
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MSIL4 acts redundantly with MSIL2 to regulate secondary cell wall formation in interfascicular fibers. 2. Its RNA-binding activity via RRM domains is essential in vivo for developmental and stem-wall phenotypes. 3. MSIL4 immunoprecipitates GXM1 and GXM3 mRNAs, linking Musashi-like translational control to glucuronoxylan methylation machinery.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Lignin; glucuronoxylan with 4-O-methylated glucuronic acid decorations
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Normal stem development; heat stress used for stress-granule localization assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pendant inflorescence stem when lost with MSIL2; reduced lignin deposition; altered SCW architecture; increased biomass digestibility
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Secondary cell wall mechanical support and biomass recalcitrance
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native MSIL4 promoter for GFP-tagged complementation and RIP constructs
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Double-mutant analysis; complementation; RRM-mutant rescue failure; in vitro RNA-binding assay; GFP-RIP; AP-MS interactome; stem histochemistry; lignin quantification; MALDI-TOF xylan oligosaccharide analysis; proteomics
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Widely expressed; enriched in interfascicular fiber and xylem vessel cells of stem
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MSIL4 is the directly assayed RNA-binding MSIL paralog in vitro and in RIP. The paper positions MSIL2/4 as post-transcriptional repressors of glucuronoxylan biosynthetic machinery accumulation.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Interfascicular fiber secondary cell walls of inflorescence stem
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignified secondary wall formation and restrains glucuronoxylan 4-O-methylation by repressing accumulation/activity of xylan methylation machinery
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GEO GSE223732; quantitative proteomics: ProteomeXchange PXD040207; PXD040020
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Widely expressed; high in interfascicular fiber and xylem vessel cells

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Glucuronoxylan decoration; 4-O-methylation of glucuronic acid side chains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates glucuronic acid substituents on glucuronoxylan to form 4-O-methyl-glucuronic acid decorations
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Glucuronoxylan carrying glucuronic acid (GlcA) substituents; methyl donor not specified in paper
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a rate-limiting methylation step in vivo; paper states GXM-mediated methylation is rate-limiting and highlights GXM3
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MSIL4 mRNA-binding association; MSIL2/4 regulatory network
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Quantitative proteomics: PXD040207; PXD040020; RNA-seq: GSE223732
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overaccumulation is associated with glucuronoxylan hypermethylation, reduced lignin deposition, altered SCW architecture, and increased saccharification in msil2/4 background
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Contributes to secondary cell wall architecture, interactions with lignin, and biomass recalcitrance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate to tune xylan methylation and biomass processing; should be modulated carefully because excessive methylation may impair wall architecture and lignification
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secondary cell wall of interfascicular fibers in inflorescence stem
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GXM3 protein overaccumulates in msil2/4 stems without major mRNA increase, indicating post-transcriptional control. 2. Increased GXM activity correlates with elevated MeGlcA/GlcA ratio on xylan. 3. GXM3 mRNA is immunoprecipitated by MSIL4 in an RRM-dependent manner, implicating translational repression by MSIL proteins.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in stem secondary-wall context; exact tissue pattern not specifically reported in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Quantitative stem proteomics; MALDI-TOF analysis of xylanase-released oligosaccharides; RNA immunoprecipitation with MSIL4-GFP; mutant/complemented line comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper emphasizes GXM3 as the likely major determinant of increased xylan methylation in msil2/4; mRNA levels are unchanged, supporting translational control.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes glucuronoxylan 4-O-methylation; increased accumulation/activity correlates with hypermethylated xylan in msil2/4

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Glucuronoxylan decoration; 4-O-methylation of glucuronic acid side chains
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates glucuronic acid substituents on glucuronoxylan
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not directly established here for GXM1 alone; family implicated in rate-limiting methylation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Quantitative proteomics: PXD040207; PXD040020; RNA-seq: GSE223732
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely contributes to xylan hypermethylation-associated wall changes when overaccumulated
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Secondary cell wall structural tuning and lignin interaction
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for biomass-quality engineering in combination with other xylan-decoration genes
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Secondary cell wall of inflorescence stem fibers
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GXM1 mRNA is bound by MSIL4 in an RRM-dependent RIP assay. 2. GXM1 protein tends to overaccumulate in msil2/4 stems, just below the preset proteomic significance threshold. 3. GXM1 likely contributes with GXM3 to increased xylan methylation in the mutant.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA immunoprecipitation; proteomics trend analysis; MALDI-TOF xylan methylation phenotype inference
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because direct MSIL4-GXM1 mRNA association was shown and the discussion/model implicates GXM1/3 in the phenotype, although GXM1 protein change did not pass the main significance cutoff.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes glucuronoxylan 4-O-methylation in stem secondary cell walls
